text.title: ads-meta-reporting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ads-meta-reporting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Changing the creative when CVR is the low metric.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CTR is a creative metric; CVR is a landing page metric. Producing new ads when the click-to-conversion rate is broken wastes creative budget and delays the actual fix.

failure-mode.description: Running weekly analysis on vanity metrics (likes, reach, impressions).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vanity metrics do not correlate with revenue. Optimizing for reach at the expense of CPA produces popular ads that lose money.

failure-mode.description: Comparing performance across periods with different external conditions (holidays, launches, seasonality).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 30% CPA increase week-over-week may be a Black Friday effect, not a campaign issue. Compare same-day-of-week, same-week-of-year, or use a holdout cell to isolate platform effects.

failure-mode.description: Producing a report with no action items.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A report without decisions is overhead. Every weekly analysis must produce at least one concrete action: scale, pause, test new creative, refresh audience.
